description: Phase 1: Code quality optimization - Extract duplicate functions, unify error handling, add logging, remove unused code
approach: This solution implements Phase 1 of the learn:plan optimization plan by: 1) Extracting the lastJsonObjectFromText function that appears 3 times (lines 281, 423, 606) into a reusable json-parser.js utility, 2) Creating error-handler.js for unified error handling patterns, 3) Adding logger.js for execution logging and debugging, 4) Refactoring plan.md to use these utilities, 5) Removing unused code and documentation. This reduces code by ~100 lines net (220 removed, 120 added) while improving maintainability and debuggability.

tasks.title: Create json-parser.js utility
tasks.description: Extract the lastJsonObjectFromText function that is duplicated 3 times in plan.md (lines 281, 423, 606) into a reusable utility module.

modification_points.change: Create utility module with lastJsonObjectFromText function

implementation: Create .claude/commands/learn/_internal/json-parser.js file
Copy the lastJsonObjectFromText function implementation from plan.md line 281-300
Add JSDoc documentation explaining the function's purpose and parameters
Add module.exports to export the function
Ensure the function handles: empty input, line-by-line JSON parsing (last valid JSON), code-fenced JSON blocks (```json```), and throws descriptive errors

unit: Test with empty string input - should throw error
Test with single-line JSON - should parse correctly
Test with multi-line output with JSON on last line - should extract last JSON
Test with code-fenced JSON block - should extract from fences
Test with noisy output before/after JSON - should tolerate and parse

criteria: json-parser.js file created in .claude/commands/learn/_internal/
lastJsonObjectFromText function exported with proper JSDoc
Function handles all 5 test cases: empty, single-line, multi-line, fenced, noisy
Module can be required and used by other files
verification: Check file exists: .claude/commands/learn/_internal/json-parser.js
Verify function signature matches original implementation
Run existing test suite: node ccw/tests/learn-doc-json-parse-robustness.test.js
Test require() from plan.md context

commit.message_template:
refactor(learn): extract lastJsonObjectFromText to json-parser utility

Extract duplicated JSON parsing function (3 occurrences) into reusable
utility module for better maintainability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tasks.title: Create error-handler.js utility
tasks.description: Create unified error handling utilities for safe file reading and command execution with JSON parsing.

modification_points.change: Create utility module with safeReadJson and safeExecJson functions

implementation: Create .claude/commands/learn/_internal/error-handler.js file
Import lastJsonObjectFromText from json-parser.js
Implement safeReadJson(path, defaultValue) function that wraps Read() with try-catch
Implement safeExecJson(command, description) function that wraps Bash() + lastJsonObjectFromText with try-catch
Add JSDoc documentation for both functions
Add module.exports to export both functions
Ensure descriptive error messages include context (file path, command description)

unit: Test safeReadJson with existing file - should parse JSON
Test safeReadJson with missing file and default value - should return default
Test safeReadJson with missing file and no default - should throw error
Test safeExecJson with valid command - should parse JSON output
Test safeExecJson with failing command - should throw descriptive error

criteria: error-handler.js file created in .claude/commands/learn/_internal/
safeReadJson and safeExecJson functions exported with JSDoc
safeReadJson handles 3 cases: success, missing with default, missing without default
safeExecJson provides descriptive error messages with command context
Module can be required and used by plan.md
verification: Check file exists: .claude/commands/learn/_internal/error-handler.js
Verify both functions have proper error handling
Test require() from plan.md context
Verify error messages include context information

commit.message_template:
refactor(learn): add error-handler utility for unified error handling

Create safeReadJson and safeExecJson utilities to standardize error
handling patterns across learn commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tasks.title: Create logger.js utility
tasks.description: Add logging mechanism for learn workflow execution to improve debuggability.

modification_points.change: Create Logger class with console and file output

implementation: Create .claude/commands/learn/_internal/logger.js file
Implement Logger class with constructor(sessionId)
Add log(level, message, data) method that writes to console and file
Add convenience methods: info(), warn(), error(), debug()
Use emoji prefixes for console output: ℹ️ info, ⚠️ warn, ❌ error, 🔍 debug
Write logs to .workflow/learn/sessions/{sessionId}/execution.log in JSONL format
Handle log write failures gracefully (ignore errors)
Add module.exports to export Logger class

unit: Test Logger instantiation with session ID
Test info() logs to console with correct prefix
Test log file is created and appended to
Test log entries are valid JSON
Test log write failures are handled gracefully
commands: node -e "const {Logger} = require('./.claude/commands/learn/_internal/logger'); const l = new Logger('test'); l.info('test'); console.log('OK')"

criteria: logger.js file created in .claude/commands/learn/_internal/
Logger class exported with 4 methods: info, warn, error, debug
Console output includes emoji prefixes
Log file written in JSONL format to session directory
Log write failures do not crash the application
verification: Check file exists: .claude/commands/learn/_internal/logger.js
Verify Logger class can be instantiated
Test console output includes emojis
Verify log file is created and contains valid JSONL
Test with non-existent session directory (should not crash)

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): add logger utility for execution tracking

Add Logger class with console and file output to improve debuggability

tasks.title: Refactor plan.md to use utilities
tasks.description: Update plan.md to use the new utility modules, removing duplicate code and unifying error handling.

modification_points.change: Add require statements for json-parser, error-handler, logger

modification_points.target: All try-catch blocks for file reading

modification_points.change: Initialize Logger instance and add logging at key steps

implementation: Add require statements at the top of the JavaScript sections in plan.md: const { lastJsonObjectFromText } = require('./_internal/json-parser'); const { safeReadJson, safeExecJson } = require('./_internal/error-handler'); const { Logger } = require('./_internal/logger');
Remove the lastJsonObjectFromText function definition at line 281-300
Remove the lastJsonObjectFromText function definition at line 423-437
Remove the lastJsonObjectFromText function definition at line 606-620
Find all try-catch blocks that read JSON files and replace with safeReadJson(path, defaultValue)
Find all Bash() calls followed by lastJsonObjectFromText() and replace with safeExecJson(command, description)
After sessionId is created, add: const logger = new Logger(sessionId);
Add logger.info() calls at key steps: profile loaded, plan generation started, validation completed, plan accepted
Add logger.warn() calls for warnings: agent generation failed, validation warnings
Add logger.error() calls for errors: validation failures, agent failures
Ensure all logging includes relevant context data

unit: Test plan.md can require all three utility modules
Test plan.md executes without errors with new utilities
Test logger creates execution.log in session directory
Test error handling works correctly with safeReadJson and safeExecJson

criteria: All 3 require statements added to plan.md
All 3 lastJsonObjectFromText definitions removed (60 lines)
All try-catch file reads replaced with safeReadJson (estimated 10 occurrences)
All Bash + JSON parse calls replaced with safeExecJson (estimated 3 occurrences)
Logger initialized after session creation
At least 5 logger.info() calls added at key steps
execution.log file created in session directory
Plan.md executes successfully with /learn:plan command
verification: Grep for 'function lastJsonObjectFromText' in plan.md - should return 0 results
Grep for 'require.*json-parser' in plan.md - should find import
Grep for 'require.*error-handler' in plan.md - should find import
Grep for 'require.*logger' in plan.md - should find import
Run /learn:plan command and verify no errors
Check .workflow/learn/sessions/{session-id}/execution.log exists
Verify log contains info/warn/error entries

exploration_context.project_structure: Claude-Code-Workflow is a JSON-driven multi-agent development framework with CLI orchestration. The learn workflow is located in .claude/commands/learn/ with supporting utilities in _internal/ subdirectory.
exploration_context.patterns: The codebase uses markdown-based command definitions with embedded JavaScript. Utility functions are extracted to _internal/ directory. Error handling follows try-catch patterns. Testing uses Node.js test runner.
exploration_context.integration_points: The plan.md command integrates with learn-planning-agent via ccw cli, uses learn-plan-validator.js for validation, and creates session files in .workflow/learn/sessions/. The new utilities will be used by plan.md and potentially other learn commands (profile.md also has duplicate lastJsonObjectFromText).
